rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renotice Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Answers To Pltf'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 15, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY Renotice Of Motion To Compel Further Answers To Plaintiff's Second Set Of Special Interrogatories. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO 2ND SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THIS SHOULD BE CONSIDERED AFTER DETERMINATION OF DEFENDANT'S TIMELY MOTION TO BIFURCATE SINCE THESE INTERROGATORIES RELATED TO PROP. 65 CIVIL PENALTIES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LUCAS VS. RECOMMIND, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Recommind, Inc¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 15, 2011, line 11, Defendant Recommind, Inc.¿s Motion For Summary Judgment. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS THE CAUSE OF THE SALE AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS AN EXEMPT OUTSIDE SALESPERSON. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LUCAS VS. RECOMMIND, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mary Lucas¿S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 15, 2011, line 11, Plaintiff Mary Lucas Motion For Summary Adjudication. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BURDEN PER CCP SECTION 437c(f)(1) AND (p)(1). ALSO, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PRESENT ANY COMPETENT EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THE PRECISE AMOUNT OF PLAINTIFF'S DAMAGES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PANDA RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. et al VS. PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents On Pltfs' Privelige Log
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 15, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANTS PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP, PAULA WEBER Motion To Compel Production Of Documents On Plaintiffs' Privilege Log. DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ON PLAINTIFFS PRIVILEGE LOG IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE SUFFICIENT PRIVILEGE LOGS SPECIFYING THE EXACT NATURE OF THE COMMUNICATION AND WHY THE PRIVILEGE APPLIES. SINCE PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN THE MOTION IS GRANTED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: HILLARY A HERNING VS. ALLERGAN, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continued Hearing On Allergan'S Demurrer And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 15, 2011, line 12, DEFENDANT ALLERGAN, INC. Demurrer And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT APPLICATION OF THE DISCOVERY RULE, THE CLAIMS ARE NOT AN IMPROPER ATTEMPT TO ENFORCE THE FDCA, AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECEIT AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION ARE PLED WITH REQUISITE PARTICULARITY. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IS DENIED. =(302/LMG)
